name: Senderos del Carbón Loop
name_original: Pola de Laviana a Ciañu, Senderos del Carbón — circular por el paseo fluvial del Nalón
description: Cycle a difficult 26.0-mile (41.8 km) route from Pola de Laviana to Ciañu, exploring coal trails and the Nalón river in Asturias.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Laviana, Spain, offers routes through diverse landscapes, blending industrial heritage with natural beauty. The region is characterized by the Nalón Valley, which features a mix of mountain scenery and remnants of its mining past, and borders the Redes Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. This area includes glacial valleys, jagged peaks, and dense forests, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Elevation gains are common, particularly in routes extending into the Redes Natural Park. Best touring cycling routes around Laviana The most popular touring cycling route is Senderos del Carbón Loop, a 26.0 miles (41.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 29 minutes to complete. This route utilizes former mining infrastructure, offering a unique blend of industrial history and natural surroundings. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bike loop from Pola de Laviana, a difficult 24.4 miles (39.2 km) path. This route explores the varied terrain surrounding Pola de Laviana, featuring significant elevation changes and scenic views of the Nalón Valley. Local touring cyclists also love the Bike loop from La Pola Llaviana / Pola de Laviana, a 17.3 miles (27.9 km) trail leading through the hills and valleys around Pola de Laviana, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Touring cycling around Laviana is defined by converted rail-trails, the mountainous terrain of the Nalón Valley, and the dense forests of the Redes Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from routes on former mining paths to more challenging climbs. The routes in Laviana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Laviana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Laviana?
A: Touring cycling around Laviana offers a diverse landscape. You'll find routes that utilize former mining infrastructure, known as the 'Senderos del Carbón', providing a unique blend of industrial history and natural surroundings. The area also features the mountainous terrain of the Nalón Valley and dense forests of the Redes Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. Expect varied terrain, from converted rail-trails to more challenging climbs with significant elevation gains, especially when venturing into the natural park.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Laviana area?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes available around Laviana, offering a wide range of options for different ability levels. The komoot community has explored these routes extensively, with over 300 touring cyclists using komoot to navigate the area's varied terrain.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly touring cycling routes in Laviana?
A: While many routes around Laviana are rated as difficult due to elevation changes, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. The region's converted rail-trails, like sections of the 'Senderos del Carbón', often provide more accessible options for those seeking a less strenuous ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029985704/senderos-del-carbon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senderos del Carbón Loop&lt;/a&gt;, while rated difficult overall, incorporates these historical paths.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while cycling in Laviana?
A: The Laviana area is rich in natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can explore the stunning Redes Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, known for its glacial valleys, karst caves, and dense forests. Specific natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuensanta Natural Mineral Spring&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5097008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojo de Buey (L'Arcu de la Finiesta)&lt;/a&gt; at Peña Mea, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570810" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pendón River Gorges&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, consider routes passing by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coto Bello Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1074608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urbiés Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Laviana?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Laviana are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029985704/senderos-del-carbon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senderos del Carbón Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31488167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Pola de Laviana&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40007341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from La Pola Llaviana / Pola de Laviana&lt;/a&gt;. These circular routes make planning your ride convenient.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Laviana?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Laviana is generally during the spring and autumn months when the weather is milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, though some days might be warmer, especially in the valleys. Winter cycling is possible, but routes in higher elevations or within the Redes Natural Park may be affected by snow or colder conditions.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Laviana?
A: The touring cycling routes around Laviana are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars from multiple reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique blend of industrial heritage along the 'Senderos del Carbón' and the breathtaking natural beauty of the Nalón Valley and Redes Natural Park. The varied terrain and scenic views are frequently highlighted as major draws.

Q: Are there any routes that showcase Laviana's industrial heritage?
A: Absolutely. The 'Senderos del Carbón' (Coal Trails) are a prime example, transforming former mining infrastructure into spectacular cycling routes. These paths allow you to explore the region's industrial past amidst natural settings, often featuring remnants of mining activity such as old train tracks and industrial bridges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029985704/senderos-del-carbon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senderos del Carbón Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great way to experience this unique aspect of Laviana.

Q: Is there a famous long-distance cycling path nearby, like the Senda del Oso?
A: Yes, the renowned &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Senda_del_Oso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt; (Bear's Path) is located nearby. This 50-kilometer rail-trail, built on an old mining railway, is ideal for both cycling and hiking. It guides visitors through lush forests, impressive gorges, and crystalline rivers, and even offers a chance to observe brown bears in a fenced enclosure. It's a fantastic option for a longer, scenic ride.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, Laviana offers several challenging routes, particularly those that venture into the Redes Natural Park. These routes feature significant elevation gains and panoramic views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31487678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campa Espinera loop from Pola de Laviana&lt;/a&gt; involves substantial climbing, making it suitable for experienced touring cyclists looking for a demanding ride.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the cycling routes in Laviana?
A: Pola de Laviana, as the main town in the area, offers various amenities including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. Many cycling routes either start from or pass through Pola de Laviana, making it a convenient base for your touring cycling adventures. You'll find opportunities to refuel and relax after your rides.

Q: What is the typical duration for a touring cycling route in Laviana?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes in Laviana varies significantly based on distance, elevation, and your cycling pace. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029985704/senderos-del-carbon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senderos del Carbón Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 26 miles (41.8 km), typically takes around 3 hours and 29 minutes. A shorter route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40007341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from La Pola Llaviana / Pola de Laviana&lt;/a&gt;, at 17.3 miles (27.9 km), can be completed in about 2 hours and 20 minutes.
tips: In Pola de Laviana there are several examples of Casas Indianas that are worth seeing. One of the most famous is the House of the Lions, which is located in the center of town. This large house was built at the beginning of the 20th century by a wealthy Asturian emigrant who had made his fortune in Cuba. Another example is the Casa de los Caballos, also built at the beginning of the 20th century. This house has a mix of styles, including Art Nouveau and Neo-Renaissance, and is known for its ornate balconies and loggias.
A visit to the church of San Esteban is worth it. It is a beautiful church located in the heart of the town. It was built in the 16th century and is a great example of Asturian baroque architecture.

The Monument to Los Maquis was erected in honor of the anti-Franco guerrillas known as "maquis" who operated in the area during and after the Spanish Civil War (1936-1939). The maquis were groups of combatants who continued to resist Francisco Franco's regime after the war officially ended. They operated in the mountains and forests of Asturias, Galicia, and other regions of Spain, carrying out acts of sabotage and guerrilla warfare against Franco's forces.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Pola de Laviana to Ciañu, Coal Trails?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in Pola de Laviana, which serves as the starting and ending point for this circular route. As the capital of the municipality, Pola de Laviana offers various parking options within the town.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is outdoors, the route is designed for touring bicycles and covers a significant distance with challenging elevation. It's generally not recommended for dogs to accompany cyclists on such a demanding route, especially given the paved sections and potential for traffic. If you're looking for dog-friendly options in the wider area, the easier Nalón river path might be more suitable for a stroll with your dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring and autumn months when the weather is milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, but be prepared for potentially warmer temperatures. Winter might present challenges with colder weather and possibly wet conditions in the mountainous terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Pola de Laviana to Ciañu, Coal Trails route. It is a public trail network.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging touring cycling route typically takes around 3.5 hours to complete. However, this duration can vary depending on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and the pace you maintain.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this route is categorized as a "difficult" bike ride requiring "very good fitness." With a significant elevation gain of over 1,000 meters and a distance of nearly 42 kilometers, it is not suitable for beginners or young families. More experienced touring cyclists will find it rewarding.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is primarily mountainous and undulating, with a substantial ascent and descent. While much of the route consists of paved roads, the significant elevation changes mean you'll encounter demanding climbs. The trail blends historical mining landscapes with natural beauty along the Nalón river.

faqs.question: What historical and natural sights can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of industrial heritage and natural beauty. You can expect to see landscapes shaped by the region's coal mining history, picturesque mountainous scenery, and views of the Nalón river. Notable highlights include the Indiano Houses of Pola de Laviana, the Church of San Esteban de Ciañu and Carbones Nueva S.A., and the Monument to the Maquis. You might also encounter charming villages and historical bridges.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options along the route?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Pola de Laviana, you'll find various cafes, pubs, and accommodation options there. Ciañu, another village on the route, also offers some amenities. It's advisable to check opening times, especially in smaller villages, and carry sufficient water and snacks for the ride.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several other paths. You will find it intersects with parts of the Asturian Way, Paséu fluvial del Nalón, Senda verde Campa Cimera - La Colladiella, and Senda verde Altu la Vara - El Sutu, among others, integrating into the broader network of trails in the region.

faqs.question: What should I pack for cycling this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should pack essentials like sufficient water, energy snacks, a basic bike repair kit (including a spare tube and pump), appropriate cycling attire for varying weather, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. A fully charged phone and a navigation device with the route downloaded are also highly recommended.

waypoints.name: Indiano Houses of Pola de Laviana
Church of San Esteban de Ciañu and Carbones Nueva S.A.
